title: Add security and correctness linters to golangci-lint
description:
# GitHub Issue #146: Extend golangci-lint configuration with security and correctness linters

**Category**: code quality / security | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: The current golangci-lint configuration only enables 6 linters (`errcheck`, `gocritic`, `govet`, `ineffassign`, `staticcheck`, `unused`). For a Kubernetes operator project with HTTP clients, error wrapping, and concurrent reconciliation logic, several important linters are missing. The `.golangci.yml` configuration should be extended with the following additional linters:

| Linter | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `gosec` | Detects security anti-patterns (hardcoded credentials, weak crypto, unsafe operations) |
| `bodyclose` | Catches unclosed HTTP response bodies that cause memory leaks |
| `nilerr` | Flags functions that return `nil` instead of the actual error |
| `errorlint` | Enforces correct `errors.Is` / `errors.As` usage (Go 1.13+ error wrapping) |
| `exhaustive` | Ensures switch statements over enum types cover all cases |
| `noctx` | Catches HTTP requests made without a `context.Context` (missing timeout/cancellation) |

Proposed `.golangci.yml` linters section:

```yaml
linters:
  default: none
  enable:
    # Existing
    - errcheck
    - gocritic
    - govet
    - ineffassign
    - staticcheck
    - unused
    # New
    - gosec
    - bodyclose
    - nilerr
    - errorlint
    - exhaustive
    - noctx
```

**Motivation**: The existing linter set covers general correctness but misses security-relevant patterns (`gosec`), resource leaks (`bodyclose`, `noctx`), and error handling correctness (`nilerr`, `errorlint`, `exhaustive`). These are particularly important in operator code where leaked connections degrade cluster health over time, swallowed errors cause silent reconciliation failures, and incomplete switch statements over condition types lead to unhandled states. Adding these linters catches entire categories of bugs at the lint stage rather than in production.

**Affected Areas**:
- `.golangci.yml`
- Go source files that produce new lint findings (to be fixed as part of this issue or tracked separately)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `.golangci.yml` enables `gosec`, `bodyclose`, `nilerr`, `errorlint`, `exhaustive`, and `noctx`
- [ ] All existing Go source files pass the extended linter configuration (fix any new findings)
- [ ] CI lint job continues to pass with the updated configuration
- [ ] No existing linters are removed or disabled
- [ ] Any `nolint` directives added to suppress false positives include a justification comment

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/146

stories.title: Developer gets security anti-pattern feedback during linting
stories.want: gosec to flag security anti-patterns (hardcoded credentials, weak crypto, unsafe operations) during local lint runs
stories.so_that: security issues are caught at development time rather than in code review or production
criteria: Running `make lint` reports gosec findings for any new code with security anti-patterns
Existing `//nolint:gosec` directive in `operators/keystone/internal/testutil/envtest_setup.go` for `InsecureSkipVerify` is preserved with its justification comment
No new gosec false positives are introduced that require suppression in production code
crypto/rand usage in reconcile_fernet.go is accepted by gosec (not flagged as insecure randomness)

stories.title: Developer gets error handling correctness feedback during linting
stories.want: errorlint and nilerr to enforce correct Go 1.13+ error wrapping patterns and prevent swallowed errors
stories.so_that: error wrapping with errors.Is/errors.As works correctly and nil-instead-of-error bugs are caught before they cause silent reconciliation failures
criteria: errorlint validates that fmt.Errorf uses %w for error wrapping
errorlint validates that errors.Is is used instead of direct error comparison
nilerr flags any function that checks err != nil but returns nil instead of the error
All existing error handling patterns across internal/common and operators/keystone pass both linters without modification

stories.title: Developer gets resource leak and exhaustiveness feedback during linting
stories.want: bodyclose, noctx, and exhaustive linters to catch HTTP response body leaks, missing context propagation, and incomplete switch statements
stories.so_that: future code additions that introduce HTTP clients or enum switches are immediately flagged if they have resource leaks or missing cases
criteria: bodyclose is enabled and would flag unclosed HTTP response bodies in any future code
noctx is enabled and would flag HTTP requests without context.Context in any future code
exhaustive validates that switch statements on enum types (e.g., PipelinePosition) cover all defined values
The existing switch on PipelinePosition in internal/common/plugins/plugins.go passes exhaustive checks

stories.title: CI enforces extended lint configuration on all pull requests
stories.want: the CI lint job to automatically enforce all 12 linters (6 existing + 6 new) on every pull request
stories.so_that: no code with security, correctness, or resource leak issues can be merged without explicit justification
criteria: CI lint job passes with the extended configuration without any workflow file changes
make lint runs successfully across internal/common, operators/keystone, and operators/c5c3
Generated code (zz_generated.*.go) remains excluded from all lint checks
No new CI job timeout issues from the additional linters

stories.title: Nolint suppressions are transparent and auditable
stories.want: every nolint directive to include a justification comment explaining why suppression is necessary
stories.so_that: suppressions are deliberate decisions rather than shortcuts, and can be reviewed for continued validity
criteria: All existing nolint directives follow the `//nolint:linter-name // reason` format
Any new nolint directives added include a justification comment
No blanket nolint directives (without specific linter names) are added
The existing `//nolint:gosec // envtest self-signed cert` in envtest_setup.go is preserved unchanged

requirements.description: The .golangci.yml configuration SHALL enable gosec, bodyclose, nilerr, errorlint, exhaustive, and noctx linters
requirements.rationale: These linters catch security anti-patterns, resource leaks, error handling bugs, and incomplete switch statements that are critical for operator reliability

scenarios.name: All six new linters appear in the enable list
scenarios.when: the .golangci.yml file is parsed by golangci-lint v2.11.4
scenarios.then: all six linters (gosec, bodyclose, nilerr, errorlint, exhaustive, noctx) are recognized and active
and_then: golangci-lint --list reports all 12 linters as enabled

scenarios.name: Configuration uses v2 format correctly
scenarios.when: the updated .golangci.yml is loaded
scenarios.then: golangci-lint does not emit configuration warnings or deprecation notices

scenarios.name: New linters are grouped separately from existing linters
scenarios.when: a developer reads .golangci.yml
scenarios.then: new linters are clearly distinguished from existing ones via comments
and_then: existing linter order and grouping is preserved

requirements.description: No existing linters SHALL be removed or disabled from the configuration
requirements.rationale: Removing existing linters would reduce coverage and could reintroduce previously caught bugs

scenarios.name: All six existing linters remain enabled
scenarios.when: the updated .golangci.yml is compared with the current configuration
scenarios.then: errcheck, gocritic, govet, ineffassign, staticcheck, and unused are still present in the enable list
and_then: no linter from the original configuration has been moved to a disable list

scenarios.name: Existing exclusion paths are preserved
scenarios.when: the exclusions section is examined
scenarios.then: the zz_generated.*\.go$ path exclusion is still present
and_then: no existing exclusion rules have been modified or removed

scenarios.name: Run timeout is preserved
scenarios.when: the run section is examined
scenarios.then: timeout remains 5m

requirements.description: All Go source files across all modules SHALL pass the extended lint configuration without errors
requirements.rationale: The extended linters must not break the existing codebase; any violations must be fixed as part of this change

scenarios.name: internal/common module passes all linters
scenarios.when: golangci-lint run ./... is executed from internal/common/
scenarios.then: the command exits with code 0 and reports no errors
and_then: all new linters are exercised against the module's Go files

scenarios.name: operators/keystone module passes all linters
scenarios.when: golangci-lint run ./... is executed from operators/keystone/
scenarios.then: the command exits with code 0 and reports no errors
and_then: the existing //nolint:gosec directive in envtest_setup.go is sufficient for the gosec finding

scenarios.name: operators/c5c3 module passes all linters
scenarios.when: golangci-lint run ./... is executed from operators/c5c3/
scenarios.then: the command exits with code 0 and reports no errors

requirements.description: Generated code matching zz_generated.*.go SHALL remain excluded from all lint checks including the new linters
requirements.rationale: Generated code (deepcopy functions from controller-gen) cannot be modified and should not produce lint noise

scenarios.name: Generated deepcopy files are excluded
scenarios.when: golangci-lint runs on a module containing zz_generated.deepcopy.go files
scenarios.then: no findings are reported from those files
and_then: the exclusion applies to all 12 linters including the 6 new ones

scenarios.name: Non-generated files are still checked
scenarios.when: golangci-lint runs on a module with both generated and hand-written Go files
scenarios.then: hand-written files are checked by all enabled linters
and_then: only the zz_generated pattern is excluded, not other generated artifacts

requirements.description: Any nolint directives added to suppress false positives SHALL include a justification comment specifying the linter name and reason
requirements.rationale: Unjustified suppressions hide real issues and make future audits impossible; the existing codebase convention requires //nolint:linter // reason format

scenarios.name: New nolint directive includes linter name and reason
scenarios.when: a new nolint directive is added to suppress a false positive
scenarios.then: the directive follows the format //nolint:linter-name // reason explaining why
and_then: the justification is specific enough to evaluate whether the suppression is still valid in future

scenarios.name: Blanket nolint without linter name is rejected
scenarios.when: a //nolint directive without a specific linter name is considered
scenarios.then: it is not added; instead the specific linter name is included

scenarios.name: Existing nolint directives are preserved unchanged
scenarios.when: the existing //nolint:gosec // envtest self-signed cert directive is reviewed
scenarios.then: it remains unchanged at operators/keystone/internal/testutil/envtest_setup.go:163

requirements.description: The make lint target SHALL pass with the extended configuration across all modules
requirements.rationale: make lint is the single entry point for both local development and CI; it must work end-to-end

scenarios.name: make lint exits successfully
scenarios.when: make lint is executed from the repository root
scenarios.then: the command runs golangci-lint on internal/common, operators/keystone, and operators/c5c3 and exits with code 0
and_then: output shows all three modules were linted

scenarios.name: make lint respects .golangci.yml from repository root
scenarios.when: golangci-lint cd's into each module directory
scenarios.then: it finds and uses the root .golangci.yml configuration
and_then: all 12 linters are applied to each module

requirements.description: The CI lint workflow job SHALL pass with the updated .golangci.yml without requiring workflow file changes
requirements.rationale: The CI workflow pins golangci-lint v2.11.4 and runs make lint; the config change should be self-contained

scenarios.name: CI workflow uses the same make lint invocation
scenarios.when: the lint job in .github/workflows/ci.yaml is examined
scenarios.then: it still runs make lint without any modifications needed
and_then: the golangci-lint version v2.11.4 supports all 6 new linters

scenarios.name: No CI timeout increase needed
scenarios.when: make lint runs with 12 linters instead of 6
scenarios.then: the lint job completes within the existing 10-minute timeout
and_then: the run.timeout: 5m in .golangci.yml is sufficient for the additional linters

requirements.description: The CI workflow reference documentation SHALL be updated to list the new linters
requirements.rationale: docs/reference/ci-workflow.md documents the lint job and should reflect the current linter set for developer onboarding

scenarios.name: New linters documented in CI reference
scenarios.when: a developer reads docs/reference/ci-workflow.md
scenarios.then: the lint job section lists all 12 enabled linters with their purpose
and_then: the feature code CC-0059 is referenced in the documentation

scenarios.name: Existing documentation content preserved
scenarios.when: the updated docs/reference/ci-workflow.md is compared with the current version
scenarios.then: only the linter-related sections are modified; all other content is unchanged

tasks.title: Update .golangci.yml to enable gosec, bodyclose, nilerr, errorlint, exhaustive, and noctx linters (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-004)
tasks.description: Add the 6 new linters to the `linters.enable` list in `.golangci.yml`. Group new linters below existing ones with a `# Security & correctness (CC-0059)` comment. Preserve the existing linter order, exclusions section (`zz_generated.*\.go$`), and run timeout (5m). Verify the config is syntactically valid by running `golangci-lint config verify` or `golangci-lint linters` from any module directory.

tasks.title: Run extended linters on internal/common module and fix any violations (REQ-003, REQ-005)
tasks.description: Run `cd internal/common && golangci-lint run ./...` with the updated configuration. Based on codebase research: no HTTP client usage (bodyclose/noctx clean), consistent fmt.Errorf %w wrapping (errorlint clean), no nil-returns-for-errors (nilerr clean), no security anti-patterns (gosec clean), no enum switches (exhaustive clean). If any violations are found, fix the source code. If a fix is not possible (false positive), add a `//nolint:linter-name // reason` directive following the project convention from envtest_setup.go:163.

tasks.title: Run extended linters on operators/keystone module and fix any violations (REQ-003, REQ-005)
tasks.description: Run `cd operators/keystone && golangci-lint run ./...` with the updated configuration. Based on codebase research: the existing `//nolint:gosec // envtest self-signed cert` on InsecureSkipVerify in envtest_setup.go:163 should handle the known gosec finding. crypto/rand usage in reconcile_fernet.go:9 is correct. No HTTP clients (bodyclose/noctx clean). Consistent error wrapping (errorlint/nilerr clean). No enum switches in operator code (exhaustive clean). If any unexpected violations are found, fix the source code or add justified nolint directives.

tasks.title: Run extended linters on operators/c5c3 module and fix any violations (REQ-003, REQ-005)
tasks.description: Run `cd operators/c5c3 && golangci-lint run ./...` with the updated configuration. The c5c3 operator consists of a single main.go (40 lines) that calls bootstrap.Run() - no error handling patterns, no HTTP usage, no switch statements, no security-sensitive code. This should pass cleanly.

tasks.title: Update docs/reference/ci-workflow.md to document the new linters (REQ-008)
tasks.description: Update the lint job section in docs/reference/ci-workflow.md to document the extended linter set. Add a table or list of all 12 enabled linters with their purpose, grouped by category (existing general correctness linters vs new security/correctness linters). Reference CC-0059 as the feature code. Add the feature code to the frontmatter `feature:` field. Keep existing content unchanged.

test_specifications.story: Developer gets security anti-pattern feedback during linting
test_specifications.expected: golangci-lint linters output includes gosec, bodyclose, nilerr, errorlint, exhaustive, noctx

test_specifications.story: CI enforces extended lint configuration on all pull requests
test_specifications.expected: golangci-lint linters output includes errcheck, gocritic, govet, ineffassign, staticcheck, unused

test_specifications.story: Developer gets error handling correctness feedback during linting
test_specifications.expected: golangci-lint run exits with code 0 for internal/common module with all 12 linters active

test_specifications.story: Developer gets security anti-pattern feedback during linting
test_specifications.expected: golangci-lint run exits with code 0 for operators/keystone module; existing nolint:gosec on InsecureSkipVerify is sufficient

test_specifications.story: CI enforces extended lint configuration on all pull requests
test_specifications.expected: golangci-lint run exits with code 0 for operators/c5c3 module (single main.go file)

test_specifications.story: CI enforces extended lint configuration on all pull requests
test_specifications.expected: Files matching zz_generated.*.go produce no lint findings from any of the 12 linters

test_specifications.story: Nolint suppressions are transparent and auditable
test_specifications.expected: All nolint directives follow //nolint:linter-name // reason format; no blanket nolint directives exist

test_specifications.story: CI enforces extended lint configuration on all pull requests
test_specifications.expected: The CI workflow file is not modified; the lint job still runs make lint with golangci-lint v2.11.4

test_specifications.story: CI enforces extended lint configuration on all pull requests
test_specifications.expected: The lint job section lists all 12 linters (6 existing + 6 new) with descriptions and CC-0059 reference

review_criteria: All 12 linters (6 existing + 6 new) appear in .golangci.yml enable list — verify by running golangci-lint linters from any module
No existing linters removed: errcheck, gocritic, govet, ineffassign, staticcheck, unused all still enabled
make lint passes with exit code 0 across all 3 modules (internal/common, operators/keystone, operators/c5c3)
Generated code exclusion (zz_generated.*\.go$) is preserved and applies to new linters
Any new nolint directives follow the //nolint:linter-name // reason convention (grep -rn nolint to verify)
No modifications to .github/workflows/ci.yaml or Makefile — config change is self-contained
docs/reference/ci-workflow.md updated with all 12 linters listed and CC-0059 referenced in frontmatter
No linter-specific settings added unless required to fix a verified false positive — defaults are preferred
implementation_notes:
Architectural decision: Add linters to the existing .golangci.yml without linter-specific settings — use defaults for all 6 new linters. The golangci-lint v2 format groups settings under linters.settings.<name>, but no custom settings are needed for the initial rollout. If false positives emerge, per-linter settings can be tuned in a follow-up.

Config structure (after change):
```
.golangci.yml
├── version: "2"
├── linters
│   ├── default: none
│   ├── enable: [12 linters]
│   └── exclusions.paths: [zz_generated]
└── run.timeout: 5m
```

Lint execution flow:
```
make lint
├── cd internal/common && golangci-lint run ./...
├── cd operators/keystone && golangci-lint run ./...
└── cd operators/c5c3 && golangci-lint run ./...
```

Each module picks up the root .golangci.yml. golangci-lint v2.11.4 (pinned in CI) supports all 6 proposed linters.

Potential pitfalls:
1. If any new linter produces unexpected findings in generated test helpers or deeply nested code not covered by research, fix the code or add a justified nolint directive.
2. The exhaustive linter uses default-signifies-exhaustive=false by default — switches with a default case still need all enum values listed. The existing PipelinePosition switch lists both values, so this is fine.
3. Do not modify .github/workflows/ci.yaml or Makefile — the config change in .golangci.yml is self-contained.
